task_completion.description: Open the SQL query editor in LibreOffice Base.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Base 'Design Query' or 'SQL View' window is open and ready for input.

task_completion.description: The SQL query references the 'Customer' and 'Invoice' tables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SQL text in the editor contains references to both the 'Customer' and 'Invoice' tables.

task_completion.description: The SQL query includes pivot logic for the required years and a grand total.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SQL text shows conditional aggregation (e.g., CASE WHEN or IF statements) for the years 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, and 2013, as well as a calculation for 'Grand_Total'.

task_completion.description: The SQL query orders the results by the grand total in descending order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SQL text ends with an 'ORDER BY Grand_Total DESC' clause.

task_completion.description: The query is executed and displays the correct column headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The query result pane is visible showing columns for Country, the years (2009-2013), and Grand_Total. Note: The data rows will be empty or zeroed due to a dataset date shift; this is expected and correct as long as the headers are present.

task_completion.description: The query is saved with the name 'CountrySalesPivot'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog box shows the name 'CountrySalesPivot', or the main LibreOffice Base window lists 'CountrySalesPivot' under the Queries section.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Base GUI to create the query.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreOffice Base application interface being interacted with, rather than a terminal or external script editor.

integrity.description: The query results are displayed within the genuine LibreOffice Base interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The query results appear in the standard LibreOffice Base data grid UI, not as text pasted into a document or mocked up.

integrity.description: The agent entered the SQL query into the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The SQL code is visible inside the LibreOffice Base SQL editor text area.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected query name is verified to be 'CountrySalesPivot'.
